channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - wccftech.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for wccftech.com

item.title: EA Sports FC 26 and Call of Duty: Vanguard Lead the Back-Half of June 2026’s Xbox Game Pass Games
item.content:encoded:
Xbox has announced the second batch of Xbox Game Pass games that'll be joining its subscription service for the rest of June 2026. This second batch is meaningfully smaller than the first, though it still has a couple of big-name franchises attached. Like EA Sports FC 26, which heads to the service fresh off its extra stint on PS Plus Essential, to keep the world's biggest football game in front of as many players during football's biggest event, the FIFA World Cup.

The other notable headliner is Call of Duty: Vanguard, which feels like a soft launch for Xbox's reported plans to add older Call of Duty titles to Game Pass now that we won't be getting the brand-new releases into Xbox Game Pass Ultimate. As for the other five games either entering or moving between tiers in the service between today and the opening days of July, indie sensation RV There Yet? from last year's whirlwind of hit indie releases is more than worth checking out.

As is Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 3+4, which is the one game that's simply jumping from one tier to another, as players subscribed to the second-rung Premium tier will now be able to play it.

Here's the full list of games, when they arrive, what platforms you can find them and what tiers can access them:

  • Junkster - June 16 - Cloud, PC, Xbox Series X/S - PC Game Pass, Game Pass Ultimate
  • Call of Duty: Vanguard - June 17 - Cloud, PC, Console - Game Pass Premium, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • EA Sports FC 26 - June 18 - Cloud, PC, Console - Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • Abyssus - June 25 - Cloud, PC, Xbox Series X/S - Game Pass Premium, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • RV There Yet? - June 30 - Cloud, PC, Xbox Series X/S - Game Pass Premium, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 3+4 - July 2 - Cloud, PC, Console - Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass, now also on Game Pass Premium
  • Winds of Arcana: Ruination - July 6 - Cloud, PC, Console - Game Pass Premium, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass

It's a smaller batch than what started the month with games like Persona 5 Royal, Undisputed, and Total Chaos thrown into the service, though it's doubtful that Xbox will catch slack for a lax Game Pass month when there are much bigger fish to fry if you've a bone to pick with Team Green.

Right now, the entire industry is waiting to find out just how terrible the reported mass layoffs at Xbox will be. We know of at least two studios that are heading for closure if they can't negotiate a buyout or find a new parent company, and we know that several more are on alert that they may also be on the chopping block.

Even if there's no threat of closure, the threat of mass layoffs and project cancellations apply across the entire Xbox Games Studios portfolio. After such a high of celebrating what's coming next in the industry with all of the announcements from Summer Game Fest, the rest of June is set to end on an incredibly low, sour note for the entire industry.

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/xbox-game-pass-june-2026-games-ea-sports-fc-26-call-of-duty-vanguard/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Clive Barker’s Hellraiser: Revival Arrives October 2026 on PC and Consoles, just in time for Halloween
item.content:encoded:
Clive Barker's Hellraiser: Revival may not have been what players expected Saber Interactive and Boss Team Games to reveal last July, but considering Saber's eclectic portfolio, it's hard to expect anything from it, really. Regardless, less than a year from the first-person horror game's reveal, we have a release date to look forward, perfectly timed for Halloween with an October 8, 2026 due date.

The news came with a brand new trailer, which capped off the immense amount of gore you see in the minute-and-a-half-long trailer. Whether you're a particularly squeamish person or if you're a horror geek that loves gore-filled stories, one takeaway you'll have when the trailer ends is that everything we saw was just a teaser of what's to come.

That's not a guarantee, but it is at least evidence that Boss Team Games and Saber Interactive understand what it means to try and adapt a franchise like Hellraiser into a first-person horror with a high bar for graphical fidelity and a realistic art style. It means not pulling any punches, and so far it looks like Boss Team Games is putting its full weight behind this game.

You'll be able to pre-order the game soon, and it'll set you back €39.99 EUR/£34.99 GBP for the standard edition and €49.99 EUR | £41.99 for the deluxe edition. There will also be two collector's editions available on the game's official website, if you're so inclined.

Pre-ordering any edition of the game will also grant you two digital skins for two of the game's weapons.

When Wccftech's Francesco De Meo played Hellraiser: Revival at Gamescom 2025, he highlighted how much it feels like Saber Interactive and Boss Team Games are on the right track. "With a no-holds-barred approach to the series' most extreme elements, I feel Clive Barker's Hellraiser: Revival will be an excellent debut for the franchise in the video games space," he wrote. "Despite playing the demo in a fully lit room amid the familiar hum of chatter that defines events like Gamescom, it feelt unsettling and horrifying nonetheless, which speaks volumes about the excellent work Saber Interactive is doing."

We'll see if he got his hopes up for nothing, or if his expectations are met, or even surpassed, this coming October 8, 2026, on PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S.

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/clive-barkers-hellraiser-revival-release-date/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Ubisoft Ends Assassin’s Creed Shadows Support With Black Tides, the True Finale Hunting Naoe and Yasuke at Last
item.content:encoded:
Today, Ubisoft is releasing the final content drop update for Assassin's Creed Shadows. Patch 1.1.11, which varies in size from the 9GB of the Mac version to the 26.1GB of the Xbox version, introduces new story content, a major endgame feature, live crossover events, and a wide range of bug fixes.

First, there's a new quest called Black Tides, which serves as the true concluding chapter for protagonists Naoe and Yasuke. Two elite Templars known as the Black Cross arrive in Japan to hunt the protagonists for the chaos they've caused, though both agents may be pursuing their own hidden agendas. To unlock the quest, players must have completed the main stories of both protagonists, as well as the two previous post-launch story drops (A Critical Encounter, which introduced rogue Rufino, and A Puzzlement, which added Kane and Kinu). Notably, having finished the Claws of Awaji expansion is not required. For players who skipped the post-launch content entirely, this effectively means there is now a three-chapter story arc waiting before this final quest, making it the de facto "real" ending of the game.

Then there's Domains, the headline new feature for hardcore players, accessible at level 30. Created in-world by MOD, an Assassin hacker, as a set of combat and survival trials for Dark Animus users, it consists of 5 gameplay simulation arenas across 10 escalating difficulty levels, with the challenge designed to force players to rethink their builds: a strategy that works in one Domain may fail entirely in the next. The feature comes with its own dedicated merchant, in-game currency, and progression tree where players can purchase Animus exploits, new endgame gear sets, powerful engravings, and fan-favorite legacy weapons. Crucially, none of these rewards will ever appear in the store or the exchange, as they are exclusively earned through Domains and intended as pure bragging-rights items for the most dedicated players.

A third Animus Rift called Horizon is also now available, continuing the Eagle/Guide storyline. Players must have completed both previous Animus Rifts to access it.

Several Assassin's Creed Shadows combat adjustments are included in Patch 1.1.11, too:

  • Damage boost for Shurikens fired from the Bank Shot and Triple Threat mastery upgrades
  • Armor-destroying abilities are now half as effective against bosses
  • Enemies now take fall damage from shorter heights, increasing overall fall damage
  • Damage from enemy Poison Kunai and Explosive Grenades is reduced
  • Up to 6 Corrupted Castles are now available each season

A batch of adrenaline-related perks have been rebalanced to percentage-based values rather than fixed ones, including Adrenaline on Headshot, Adrenaline on Critical Hit, and several others. Key item buffs include:

  • Whisper of Gold Long Katana perk now grants 100% ability damage (up from 50%)
  • Ethereal Cloth Headgear perk now triggers at below 15% health (up from 10%)
  • Pink Petal Kosode and Sakura Bloom Armor now convert adrenaline to 20% health on death (up from 10%)
  • Teppo and Bow weapons can now be engraved with armor damage and armor-piercing perks

Finally, the Animus HUB is now accessed exclusively through the Shadows Memory page in the system menu, harmonizing in-menu and in-gameplay navigation. The Forge engraving list has been reordered with a new Critical Category added, and world map pins and markers now correctly persist through travel between Japan and Awaji. On that note, the patch also adds two new crossover Projects in the Animus HUB, Riptides and Undertow, which unlock themed outfits, weapons, trinkets, and other rewards. Players activate them by completing Anomalies and progressing through the reward tracks, just like previous HUB projects. These rewards span both Assassin’s Creed Shadows and the upcoming Black Flag Resynced, so progress can be made by playing Anomalies in either game. One important limitation is that the crossover Projects will not be available on Nintendo Switch 2, since the game isn't coming to the console.

Did you enjoy your time with Assassin's Creed Shadows? Let us know by leaving a comment below.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/assassins-creed-shadows-black-tides-finale/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
